hypothesis: Systematic biases in GFN2-xTB geometry optimization failures are non-random with respect to molecular topology—specifically, molecules containing strained ring systems, highly conjugated polycyclic frameworks, or reactive functional groups will exhibit disproportionately higher GFN2-xTB fragmentation rates, and training generative models on datasets that selectively exclude these chemotypes will introduce a measurable and quantifiable blind spot in the learned chemical space distribution, such that models trained on the filtered GEOM-Drugs dataset will generate molecules with statistically lower ring strain and fewer reactive substructures compared to models trained on the unfiltered dataset.
